frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment: Honestly, I think more young men should insist on prenups. We keep pretending alimony is always about justice, but in plenty of cases it has become reverse dowry, especially when marriages barely last and the financial claim is huge. There are genuine victims, of course, but the conversation around 498A misuse and one-sided laws cannot just be dismissed.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women broadly as potential financial exploiters of marriage and divorce law while centering male victimhood. It encodes the 'reverse dowry' and 'one-sided laws/498A misuse' surface manifestations in a polished, professional register.
persona_text: You are Priya. Age 34. Married with one child aged three. A woman working as a journalist in Mumbai. You are on a panel at a media industry event. A male journalist on the same panel is asked about his biggest professional achievement this year. He describes a long investigation he published. He is not asked about his child. You are asked next about your biggest professional achievement this year. Before you can answer the moderator adds Ñ and of course as a working mother how do you balance it all. You are responding to this question in front of 300 people. What you say and what you do not say and the smile you maintain while saying it is the entire content of this persona.
